---
title: "RieszReg"
subtitle: "Riesz regression for semiparametric inference"
engine: knitr
---

The `rieszreg` family estimates the Riesz representer $\alpha_0$ of a linear functional $\theta(P) = \mathbb{E}[m(Z, g_0)]$, the building block of one-step, TMLE, and DML estimators in semiparametric inference.

The meta-package `rieszreg` holds the shared abstractions: `Estimand` factories, Bregman-Riesz `LossSpec`s, the augmentation engine, the `Backend` Protocol, and the sklearn-compatible `RieszEstimator` orchestrator. Implementation packages provide concrete backends:

- [`rieszboost`](backends/boosting.qmd) — gradient boosting (xgboost / sklearn) implementing [Lee & Schuler (arXiv:2501.04871)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.04871).
- [`krrr`](backends/kernel.qmd) — closed-form kernel ridge regression following [Singh (arXiv:2102.11076)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.11076).
- [`forestriesz`](backends/forest.qmd) — random-forest moment estimation following [Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martínez, Syrgkanis (ICML 2022)](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v162/chernozhukov22a/chernozhukov22a.pdf), with honest-split confidence intervals.

R and Python predictions are bitwise-identical on the same input. Most pages present every code example in both languages; pick a tab and your choice persists across the site.

## Two ways to compose

```python
# Convenience class with sensible defaults baked in:
from rieszboost import RieszBooster
from rieszreg import ATE
booster = RieszBooster(estimand=ATE(), n_estimators=200)
booster.fit(X)

# Or compose explicitly: pick any backend, pass it to RieszEstimator
from rieszreg import RieszEstimator, ATE, SquaredLoss
from rieszboost.backends import XGBoostBackend
est = RieszEstimator(estimand=ATE(), loss=SquaredLoss(), backend=XGBoostBackend())
est.fit(X)
```

Both routes are first-class. The convenience class is just `RieszEstimator` with the backend defaulted.

## Pages

- **Concepts.** [What's a Riesz representer](intro.qmd) · [Estimands](estimands.qmd) · [Losses](losses.qmd).
- **Get started.** [Install](install.qmd) · [Quickstart](quickstart.qmd).
- **Usage.** [Tuning and Cross-Fitting](tuning-cross-fitting.qmd) · [Diagnostics](diagnostics.qmd) · [Save and load](save-and-load.qmd) · [R interface](r-interface.qmd).
- **Backends.** [Overview](backends/index.qmd) · [Boosting](backends/boosting.qmd) · [Kernel](backends/kernel.qmd) · [Forest](backends/forest.qmd).
- [Developing](developing.qmd).
- [References](references.qmd).

::: {.callout-tip title="Code execution"}
Every code block in this guide ran at build time. The numbers and figures came from the version of `rieszreg`, `rieszboost`, `krrr`, and `forestriesz` that produced this page.
:::
